top_signals.title: Iran Strikes 5 Gulf Countries as Regional Escalation Continues
top_signals.summary: Iran has opened a new front across the Gulf, striking countries that host U.S. forces and sit beside the oil, gas, and shipping infrastructure sustaining the global energy system. Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, and Jordan all reported Iranian missile or drone activity on Sunday after the United States hit a reported 140 military targets inside Iran in response to an Iranian strike on a commercial vessel near the Strait of Hormuz. The heaviest confirmed damage occurred in Kuwait, where a drone struck an offshore drilling platform operated by Kuwait…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: FirstFT: US-Iran ceasefire teeters

top_signals.title: Gold price loses its grip: World’s 50 biggest mining companies shed $228 billion in Q2
top_signals.summary: Gold's slide back below $4,000 an ounce wipes out most of mining's 2026 gains, but the diversified giants – led by a resurgent BHP – is staging a comeback.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin bulls Michael Saylor, Adam Back slam BIP-110 Ordinals proposal
top_signals.summary: The ongoing debate over a proposed temporary fork comes despite a broad downturn in Ordinals transaction activity over the last two years.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.
